review_summary: The transcript summary is sufficient for small architecture-design questions, so no additional video clip is needed.

question_bank.question: Why can three stacked 3×3 convolutions be preferable to one 7×7 convolution when channel count is fixed?
choices: They give the same receptive field with fewer parameters and more nonlinearities
They always reduce spatial size more aggressively
They remove the need for pooling layers
They force the classifier to output fewer classes
question_bank.explanation: Stacking three 3×3 layers reaches an effective 7×7 receptive field, but the stack uses fewer parameters and inserts extra nonlinear transformations, which can make the model more expressive.

question_bank.question: In a VGG-style block with 3×3 convolutions, stride 1, and padding 1, what happens to spatial resolution across the convolution stack?
choices: It is preserved until a pooling layer changes it
It is halved after every convolution
It becomes equal to the number of channels
question_bank.explanation: Stride 1 with padding 1 keeps the height and width unchanged for each 3×3 convolution. Spatial resolution typically changes later at pooling or downsampling stages.
